subject: [PATCH] mm/mincore: handle non-swap entries before !CONFIG_SWAP guard

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges a fix is needed for the swapoff path and agrees to restructure in v2
reviews.raw_body:
Ah yes, I have cc-ed stable in the reply to this email, but probably that
is not enough?

Thanks

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary: Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes noted that the patch description is missing a Cc: in the body, but mentioned Andrew's inconsistent practice of adding Cc's for Cc'd parties.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah I think a Cc: in the body is required, but then again Andrew does add Cc's
for Cc'd parties so maybe it'll be automagically sorted out.

Andrew - probably we're good here but just checking to be sure?

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the original patch moved the !IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_SWAP) guard below the non-swap-entry check as intended to prevent spurious WARNs on !CONFIG_SWAP kernels

The reviewer noted that the patch's change is unnecessary because the condition in mincore_swap() will already be false for !CONFIG_SWAP builds, and thus the WARN_ON(1) and return statement are redundant.
reviews.raw_body:
Logic LGTM so:

Reviewed-by: Lorenzo Stoakes <ljs@kernel.org>

Small note below but I think not a problem.

Thanks, Lorenzo

---

I guess fine to do this after software_is_swap() because for !CONFIG_SWAP this
will always evaluate false?

Not a big deal therefore I think.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the softleaf_is_swap() function isn't structurally gated by CONFIG_SWAP, but its callers are, and thus it will always return false on !CONFIG_SWAP builds.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, thats right. In softleaf_is_swap(), softleaf_type() returns SOFTLEAF_SWAP
iff type_num < MAX_SWAPFILES. MAX_SWAPFILES is a compile-time constant independent
of CONFIG_SWAP, so softleaf_is_swap() isn't structurally gated, but every producer
of real swap entries lives under CONFIG_SWAP.
With !CONFIG_SWAP none of those run, so softleaf_is_swap() is always false.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the patch requires formal Acked-by tag from the stable maintainers, as it contains a Fixes: line and -stable maintainers have been asked not to auto-accept such patches
reviews.raw_body:
It's required for MM patches because we've asked the -stable
maintainers to not auto-pick everything which has a Fixes:.

2026-06-03.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's mincore function where it incorrectly reports non-swap entries as non-resident on systems without swap enabled. The fix involves moving the check for !CONFIG_SWAP below the early return for non-swap entries, ensuring that only true swap entries trigger warnings and non-shmem pages follow the existing 'uptodate / non-shmem' path.

2026-06-08.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's mincore function where it incorrectly reports non-swap entries as non-resident on systems without swap enabled. The fix involves moving the check for !CONFIG_SWAP below the early return for non-swap entries, ensuring that only true swap entries trigger warnings and non-shmem pages follow the existing 'uptodate / non-shmem' path.
